markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Require every WP-009 feature/tool/primitive/UI/backend API to update or validate UserManual content during build. This MT belongs to UserManualAndNoContextOps. Make UserManual a built-in product surface that enables no-context model and future user operation.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The canonical stuck-together product term is UserManual; legacy ModelManual, Model Manual, and model_manual may remain only as declared deprecated shims with migration tests.
UserManual updates are build deliverables for every touched feature/tool/primitive/surface, not optional docs.
scope.expected_behavior: UserManualBuildUpdateRule leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: UserManualBuildUpdateRule is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 fcf9bce3. Build-update rule implemented as a three-layer enforcement substrate over src/user_manual/registry.rs (99 typed SurfaceDescriptor rows = the declared WP-009 surface inventory: ingestion 6, code-nav 6, documents 16, retrieval 5, memory 5, crdt 3, Notes/Loom 43 incl. the POST /workspaces prerequisite, usermanual 16): (1) COMPILE-TIME gate - lib test user_manual::seed::tests::every_registry_surface_is_anchor_covered_in_the_corpus fails the build when a registry surface lacks an http_route anchor in the seed corpus (spec 10.15.8: coverage in the same implementation unit); (2) DB gate - user_manual_storage_tests::mt195_every_registry_surface_has_db_coverage proves every surface has anchor + tool-entry rows in seeded PostgreSQL; (3) RUNTIME gate - user_manual_api_tests::mtdoc_every_registry_surface_exists_on_the_real_router probes all 99 documented (method,route) pairs against the REAL full api::routes router (405 or bare router-404 fails). Negative proof the gate can fire: mt195_uncovered_surface_detection_fires (delete a route anchor -> freshness uncovered_surface verdict -> reseed heals). RED-then-GREEN evidence: the compile-time gate FAILED on first run (code-nav anchors missing from knowledge-index-surface) and was fixed by adding the anchors - the rule provably blocks uncovered surfaces. New-surface law documented in registry.rs module docs: a later group lands its SurfaceDescriptor + seed coverage in the same unit or the suite is RED.

concerns_found_and_fixed: Gate fired RED on first run: /knowledge/code/* anchors missing from the knowledge-index page (sections documented them but anchors were not emitted) -> group_route_anchors(CodeNavigation) added; this is also the MT-112 closure path.

Direction-coverage gap: registry->anchor only catches uncovered surfaces; an anchor documenting a REMOVED surface needed the inverse check -> dangling_anchor verdict in freshness + the runtime router probe close it (caught the undeclared POST /workspaces anchor; fixed by declaring the prerequisite surface).
residual_risks: Axum routers cannot be introspected, so a route mounted but ABSENT from the registry is not auto-detected; mitigation: the registry is the declared build-rule surface (spec 10.15.8 makes the declaring MT responsible), and the parity gate (DEC-006) audits inventory externally.

adversarial_review_v2.summary: Follow-up adversarial review found no blocking findings after durable ModelManual-to-UserManual wording, USER_MANUAL_VERSION HBR-MAN-001 guidance, Loom route parity, full-row freshness, receipt atomicity, and fail-closed PostgreSQL test fixes.

ready_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 working tree after fcf9bce3 includes UserManual recovery fixes; validator must review working-tree diff, not only fcf9bce3.
cargo test -j 1 --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test user_manual_storage_tests -- --nocapture: PASS, 11 passed.
cargo test -j 1 --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test user_manual_api_tests -- --nocapture: PASS, 12 passed.

POSTGRES_TEST_URL=postgres://postgres@127.0.0.1:5565/handshake cargo test -j 1 --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test user_manual_content_tests -- --nocapture: PASS, 11 passed.
git diff --check: PASS, line-ending warnings only; stale standalone HBR-MAN-001 MANUAL_VERSION source/artifact scan: no hits.

hardening_closed.note: State recovered to READY_FOR_VALIDATION only. completed_by remains null; validator owns PASS/COMPLETED.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-195 requires a build-update rule that forces every WP-009 surface to add/validate UserManual coverage in the same implementation unit, with real PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority and non-mock runtime proof (spec 10.15.8). The code delivers exactly this via a three-layer enforcement substrate (compile-time anchor-coverage test, real-PostgreSQL DB coverage test, and a real-router runtime probe over all registry surfaces) plus a negative test proving the gate can fire and heal.
findings: Spec 10.15.8 in CURRENT v02.194 (10-product-surfaces.md:8151-8157) explicitly states a WP-KERNEL-009 surface without UserManual coverage 'is a build-rule defect' and coverage must land 'in the same implementation unit' — MT-195 implements precisely this rule.

Compile-time gate is real: seed.rs:1942 every_registry_surface_is_anchor_covered_in_the_corpus iterates the actual wp009_surface_registry() and asserts each (method,route) has an http_route anchor in the seed corpus; failure aborts the build.

DB gate is real PostgreSQL: tests/user_manual_storage_tests.rs:419 mt195_every_registry_surface_has_db_coverage seeds an isolated PG schema and asserts every registry surface has anchor + tool-entry rows; skip_if_no_pg! (user_manual_support.rs:133) PANICS when PG is absent, so the proof is fail-closed, not vacuously skipped.

Runtime gate is real: tests/user_manual_api_tests.rs:698 mtdoc_every_registry_surface_exists_on_the_real_router builds a real AppState (PostgresDatabase + real pool, user_manual_support.rs:95) and serves api::routes(state) on a loopback listener, then probes all registry surfaces; a bare router-404 (empty body) or 405 fails — genuine doc-vs-runtime proof against the real product router.

Negative/fire proof is real: storage_tests.rs:460 mt195_uncovered_surface_detection_fires DELETEs a route anchor via raw sqlx, asserts check_freshness() returns an UncoveredSurface verdict, then reseeds and asserts the verdict clears. The freshness logic (freshness.rs:273-290) genuinely diffs the registry against DB http_route anchors in both directions (uncovered + dangling).

Durable authority is real PostgreSQL: migration 0310_user_manual.sql creates user_manual_pages/sections/anchors/tool_entries/feature_entries/versions/legacy_aliases — no SQLite, no mock store. NoopRecorder/NoopLlmClient in the test harness are peripheral flight-recorder/LLM stand-ins, not the manual storage path.
No deferred-live escapes: grep of src/user_manual/ for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-'/*Unavailable returned zero hits.
No self-certification: lifecycle.completed_by is null, status READY_FOR_VALIDATION, hardening_closed note explicitly leaves PASS/COMPLETED to the validator.

Minor (non-blocking) aside: the MT narrative says 99 surfaces but registry.rs has 106 surface!() rows; the gate tests iterate the actual registry so coverage is enforced over the real set regardless — this is benign narrative-vs-code count drift, not a code defect.
evidence_checked: wtc-kernel-009 git show --stat fcf9bce3 + git log --grep MT-195

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
